name: Digital Artificial Insemination Gun (IB-G2)
shortDesc: Device that detects heat cycles and pregnancy in female cows, sheep, and goats, improving the success of artificial insemination.
fullDesc:
### About

This device can detect **heat cycles** and **pregnancy** in female cows, sheep, and goats, improving the success of artificial insemination.

### Development

Through a grant program in 2022, the second-generation **IB-Gun (IB-G2)** was developed with a high-resolution camera and onboard color LCD for accurate semen placement. It also includes a temperature sensor to monitor the reproductive health of livestock.
specs: High-resolution camera for accurate semen placement
Temperature sensor for reproductive health monitoring

name: Alat Pengering Batik (Batik Dryer)
client: UMKM Sinar Abadi Batik Kulon Progo
shortDesc: Drying chamber for batik fabric using water heat converted to hot air, centrally integrated and automatically controlled.
fullDesc:
### About

The batik drying chamber is a specially designed room for drying batik fabric. It utilizes a system that converts **water heat into hot air**, which is then circulated inside the chamber.

### How It Works

- Average temperature inside the drying chamber reaches **42°C**, ensuring quick and efficient drying of batik fabric
- The system is centrally integrated and can be controlled automatically

### Impact

This innovation aims to provide a solution for **UMKM Sinar Abadi Batik**, allowing them to shorten drying time, maintain production stability, and preserve the quality of the batik fabric.
specs: Water heat to hot air conversion system

name: Smart Leveling
shortDesc: Robot utilizing inertia sensors and mecanum-driven wheels to detect and measure floor surface inclination.
fullDesc:
### About

Tim mahasiswa dari Sekolah Vokasi UGM berhasil menciptakan robot pintar pemindai dan pengukur kemiringan permukaan lantai dengan teknologi **sensor inersia** dan **mechanum-driven wheel** yang mampu bergerak secara **3-DOF** (Degree of Freedom).

### Function

Robot yang dinamakan Smart Leveling dapat digunakan untuk mempersingkat waktu pekerjaan pengecekan kerataan lantai keramik tanpa mengurangi kualitas pekerjaan sehingga mengurangi risiko terjadinya kerugian pemilik proyek maupun pelaksana proyek.

### Achievement

Robot canggih ini merupakan salah satu produk unggulan dari **Sekolah Vokasi UGM** yang diikutsertakan dalam kompetisi **Program Kreativitas Mahasiswa - Karya Inovatif (PKM-KI)** dan mendapat pendanaan riset dari Dirjen Kemendikbud Ristek RI.
specs: 3-DOF movement capability (translasi maju-mundur, geser kanan-kiri, diagonal)

name: Kandang Hewan Percobaan dengan Ventilasi Individu (IVC Cage)
shortDesc: Individually Ventilated Cage (IVC) - Sistem kandang khusus hewan laboratorium dengan ventilasi udara terpisah.
fullDesc:
### About

**Kandang Hewan Percobaan dengan Ventilasi Individual (Individually Ventilated Cage/IVC)** merupakan sistem pemeliharaan hewan laboratorium yang dilengkapi ventilasi udara terpisah pada setiap kandang.

### Airflow System

Udara bersih disuplai melalui sistem **Air Handling Unit (AHU)** dan disaring menggunakan **HEPA filter** sebelum didistribusikan ke masing-masing kandang.

### Benefits

- Menjaga hewan tetap bebas dari kontaminasi mikroorganisme
- Mencegah infeksi silang antar kandang
- Memastikan kondisi lingkungan yang stabil sehingga menghasilkan data penelitian yang akurat, konsisten, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan

### Application

IVC banyak digunakan pada fasilitas penelitian biomedis, farmasi, dan laboratorium yang memelihara hewan dengan standar **Specific Pathogen Free (SPF)**.
specs: Supply port dan exhaust port otomatis

name: Kandang Hewan Percobaan Filter Udara (IVC Rack System)
shortDesc: IVC Rack System - Sistem rak kandang hewan laboratorium dengan HEPA filter untuk udara bersih.
fullDesc:
### About

**Individually Ventilated Cage (IVC)** merupakan sistem kandang khusus untuk hewan laboratorium (tikus, mencit, hamster, dll.) yang setiap kandangnya memiliki aliran udara bersih sendiri, sehingga antar kandang tidak saling mencemari.

### HEPA Filtration

Berbeda dengan kandang biasa yang udara di dalam rak bercampur, pada IVC setiap kandang mendapatkan suplai udara yang telah disaring menggunakan **HEPA Filter**.

> HEPA mampu menyaring sekitar **99,97%** partikel berukuran 0,3 mikron, sehingga bakteri, spora jamur, debu, dan sebagian besar aerosol biologis tertahan.
specs: Rak IVC dengan manifold distribusi udara
Supply dan Exhaust HEPA Filter terpisah

name: SA-6 Mortar Dropper Tactical UAV
shortDesc: Development of a tactical multirotor UAV capable of carrying and deploying mortar payloads with high stability, precision, and mission readiness for defense applications.
fullDesc:
### About

The SA-6 Mortar Dropper is a tactical multirotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) developed by Frogs Indonesia to support modern military and defense operations. Designed and manufactured in Indonesia, the platform demonstrates the capability of domestic engineering in developing reliable aerial systems for reconnaissance, tactical logistics, and precision payload delivery in complex operational environments.

### Exhibition

The UAV was officially showcased during the **Indo Defence 2025 Expo & Forum**, one of Southeast Asia's largest international defense exhibitions held at JIExpo Kemayoran, Jakarta. The exhibition highlighted the growing capabilities of Indonesia's defense industry, where the SA-6 attracted attention as an indigenous tactical drone platform capable of supporting front-line operations.

### Capabilities

The SA-6 is engineered to provide operational flexibility in areas that are difficult or dangerous for ground personnel to access. Powered by a multirotor propulsion system, the aircraft offers a **flight endurance of approximately 15-25 minutes**, making it suitable for short-range tactical missions such as battlefield reconnaissance, border surveillance, target observation, and rapid response operations.

### Payload System

One of its primary capabilities is the integration of a **60 mm lightweight mortar deployment system**, allowing the UAV to carry and release up to four mortar rounds. The payload release mechanism is designed to ensure reliable deployment while maintaining aircraft stability throughout the mission. In addition to tactical payloads, the platform is capable of carrying up to **7 kg of payload**, enabling its use for logistics support, ammunition resupply, medical equipment delivery, emergency supplies, and other mission-specific equipment where rapid aerial transportation is required.

### Navigation

The navigation system combines **GPS-based autonomous flight** capabilities with manual remote-control operation, providing operators with an intuitive and efficient user experience. The system is designed for rapid deployment, enabling military personnel to operate the aircraft with minimal setup time under demanding field conditions.

With an operational communication range of up to **15 km**, the SA-6 is capable of conducting missions several kilometers beyond the operator's position while maintaining sufficient battery capacity for a safe return flight. This range provides tactical units with enhanced situational awareness and operational reach without exposing personnel to unnecessary risk.

### Mechanical Design

Mechanically, the platform emphasizes structural rigidity, lightweight construction, ease of maintenance, and modularity. The airframe is optimized to withstand the dynamic loads generated during payload transport and deployment while maintaining stable flight characteristics. Modular mechanical interfaces also allow rapid replacement or reconfiguration of payload systems depending on mission requirements.

### My Role

As a Mechanical Design Engineer at Frogs Indonesia, I was responsible for the complete mechanical design and development of the aircraft. My responsibilities included designing structural components, frame assemblies, payload integration systems, mortar mounting mechanisms, landing gear, electronic equipment mounts, battery enclosures, and manufacturing-ready mechanical parts. The development process involved extensive 3D CAD modeling, mechanical design optimization, tolerance analysis, material selection, design for manufacturability (DFM), rapid prototyping, fabrication support, assembly validation, and iterative engineering improvements based on testing and operational feedback.

Throughout the project, I collaborated closely with multidisciplinary teams including electrical engineers, embedded systems developers, flight control engineers, and manufacturing technicians to ensure seamless integration between the mechanical structure, avionics, propulsion system, payload mechanisms, and flight controller. This collaborative engineering approach enabled the development of a robust UAV platform capable of meeting demanding operational requirements while remaining practical for production and field deployment.

### Conclusion

The SA-6 Mortar Dropper represents an important milestone in Indonesia's indigenous UAV development, demonstrating how locally engineered technologies can provide effective tactical capabilities for reconnaissance, logistics support, and precision payload deployment while strengthening the nation's defense technology ecosystem.

name: Smart Solar Finder (Prototipe)
fullDesc:
### About

Designed and developed a prototype **dual-axis solar tracking system** to demonstrate automatic photovoltaic (PV) panel positioning based on real-time sunlight detection. The prototype utilizes an **Arduino Uno**, four **Light Dependent Resistor (LDR) sensors**, and two **servo motors** to continuously adjust the panel's horizontal (azimuth) and vertical (elevation) angles, ensuring maximum solar exposure throughout the day.

### How It Works

The control algorithm compares light intensity from four directional sensors and dynamically drives both axes to minimize the difference in illumination, allowing the solar panel to maintain an optimal orientation relative to the sun. The project included electronic circuit design, embedded software development, mechanical assembly, sensor calibration, and system testing.

### Scalability

Although developed as a proof-of-concept prototype, the system architecture is highly scalable. The same tracking principle can be implemented on larger solar installations by replacing hobby servo motors with industrial actuators, stepper motors, or geared DC motors equipped with appropriate position feedback and motor drivers. The control algorithm can also be migrated to industrial-grade controllers such as STM32, PLCs, or other embedded platforms, making it suitable for commercial-scale photovoltaic tracking applications.

### Key Responsibilities

- Designed the mechanical structure for dual-axis motion
- Developed embedded firmware using Arduino (C/C++)
- Designed and assembled the electronic control system
- Implemented real-time solar tracking algorithms using four LDR sensors
- Performed sensor calibration and servo tuning to improve tracking accuracy
- Built and tested a functional prototype for performance validation
- Evaluated scalability for larger photovoltaic systems using industrial actuators and controllers

### Technologies

`Arduino Uno` `Embedded C/C++` `LDR Sensors` `Servo Motors` `Solar Energy Systems` `Mechanical Design` `Electronics Design` `Control Systems` `Rapid Prototyping` `Sensor Calibration`

name: Automatic Wood Drying Oven Control System
client: local wood craftsmen in Pandeglang, Banten, Indonesia
shortDesc: Field Implementation – Community-Based Wood Drying Automation
fullDesc:
### About

Designed and implemented an automated control system for a wood drying oven as part of a community engineering project for local wood craftsmen in **Pandeglang, Banten, Indonesia**. The system was developed to improve drying efficiency, reduce manual operation, and maintain consistent drying temperatures for higher-quality wood processing.

### Control System

The control system integrates a **digital thermostat (STC-1000)** with a relay-based electrical panel to automatically regulate the heating process. A temperature sensor continuously monitors the drying chamber and controls the gas burner through an electric solenoid valve. The blower system is equipped with an **SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier) speed controller** to optimize airflow and heat distribution, while a DC-powered ignition module provides reliable automatic burner ignition.

The temperature controller allows operators to configure the maximum drying temperature and hysteresis, enabling stable and energy-efficient operation with minimal user intervention.

### Key Responsibilities

- Designed the electrical control system and wiring architecture
- Configured automatic temperature control using a digital thermostat
- Integrated gas burner automation with an electric solenoid valve
- Implemented variable-speed blower control using an SCR controller
- Installed and tested the complete control system on-site
- Commissioned the system for daily use by local wood craftsmen

### Technologies & Components

`STC-1000 Digital Thermostat` `Relay-Based Control Panel` `Gas Solenoid Valve` `SCR AC Motor Speed Controller` `DC Ignition Module` `220 VAC Electrical Distribution` `Temperature Sensor` `Forced-Air Blower System`

### Project Impact

The automated wood drying oven has been successfully deployed and operated by local wood craftsmen in Pandeglang, Banten. The system improves temperature stability, reduces manual supervision, enhances drying consistency, and increases operational efficiency compared to conventional manual drying methods.
specs: The system was developed to improve drying efficiency
and maintain consistent drying temperatures for higher-quality wood processing
A temperature sensor continuously monitors the drying chamber
controls the gas burner through an electric solenoid valve
Configured automatic temperature control using a digital thermostat
Integrated gas burner automation with an electric solenoid valve
Implemented variable-speed blower control using an SCR controller

name: Smart NPK Analyzer
fullDesc:
### About

The **Smart NPK Analyzer Prototype** is a research and development project owned by the Faculty of Agricultural Technology (FTP), Universitas Gadjah Mada (UGM). The system is designed to provide rapid digital measurement of the primary soil nutrients - **Nitrogen (N)**, **Phosphorus (P)**, and **Potassium (K)** - through an integrated desktop instrument.

As part of the engineering support team, I contributed to the development of the prototype by assisting in the mechanical, electrical, and embedded system integration, transforming research concepts into a functional working prototype.

### Working Principle

The operator places the prepared sample into the measurement chamber and initiates the analysis through the **touchscreen interface**. The instrument performs an automated measurement sequence, ensuring consistent operating conditions during data acquisition.

The embedded control system processes sensor data to obtain the nutrient values for Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K). The results are displayed in real time on the touchscreen interface and can be printed immediately using the integrated **thermal printer**.

To improve usability, the system also provides a preliminary interpretation of the measurement results, allowing users to quickly assess the nutrient condition of the tested sample.

### My Contribution

I supported the development of this prototype by contributing to:

- Mechanical enclosure design and assembly support
- Embedded firmware development
- Sensor communication integration
- Touchscreen HMI implementation
- Thermal printer integration
- Electronic system integration
- Prototype assembly, testing, and functional validation

### Technologies

`Embedded Systems` `ESP32 Firmware Development` `RS485 Communication` `Human Machine Interface (HMI)` `Thermal Printing` `Electronics Integration` `Mechanical Design Support` `Prototype Development` `Functional Testing`

### Status

This instrument is currently in the **Research & Development (R&D)** stage. The prototype has been developed as a validation platform for future refinement, calibration, and feature expansion before wider deployment. Certain implementation details are intentionally omitted due to the ongoing research and development process.

> Note: This project is owned by the Faculty of Agricultural Technology, Universitas Gadjah Mada (FTP UGM). The information presented here highlights my engineering contributions to the prototype development and intentionally excludes proprietary technical details due to the ongoing R&D process.
specs: Sensor, HMI, embedded controller, and printer integrated into a single desktop unit
Compact desktop enclosure with modular internal architecture
Rapid digital measurement of Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K)
Easy operation with minimal user interaction

name: PET Bottle Filament Maker
shortDesc: PET Bottle Filament Maker For 3D Printing
fullDesc:
# PET Bottle Filament Maker

The **PET Bottle Filament Maker** is an innovative recycling machine developed at the **Industrial Equipment Laboratory, Department of Mechanical Engineering, Vocational College, Universitas Gadjah Mada (UGM)**. The system is designed to convert discarded polyethylene terephthalate (PET) bottles into high-quality 3D printing filament, providing an environmentally friendly solution for plastic waste management while promoting sustainable manufacturing practices.

The machine integrates multiple processing stages—including **bottle slicing, filament extrusion, cooling, and automatic filament winding**—into a single compact system. This integrated design streamlines the recycling workflow, minimizes manual handling, and improves production efficiency. By transforming plastic waste into reusable engineering material, the project supports the principles of the **circular economy** and contributes to the **United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)** by encouraging responsible consumption and sustainable production.

To ensure stable filament quality, the extrusion process is regulated using a **PID (Proportional–Integral–Derivative) temperature control system**, maintaining consistent thermal conditions throughout operation. The bottle slicer supports multiple strip-width configurations (**6 mm, 8 mm, 10 mm, and 12 mm**) to accommodate various PET bottle dimensions and optimize material feeding. An integrated **automatic winding mechanism** produces neatly spooled filament, while a **touchscreen Human-Machine Interface (HMI)** provides an intuitive interface for machine operation, monitoring, and parameter adjustment.

Developed as a **Research and Development (R&D) prototype**, the PET Bottle Filament Maker demonstrates the feasibility of converting post-consumer PET waste into valuable additive manufacturing material. The system has been designed with a **modular and scalable architecture**, allowing future enhancements in production capacity, process automation, quality control, and industrial-scale implementation.

Beyond its technical functionality, the machine serves as an educational and research platform for sustainable manufacturing, polymer recycling, mechanical system design, and embedded control engineering. The project highlights how engineering innovation can create practical solutions for environmental challenges by converting plastic waste into a valuable resource for additive manufacturing.

---

## Technologies Used

- Mechanical System Design
- Polymer Extrusion
- PET Recycling Technology
- PID Temperature Control
- Embedded Control System
- Human-Machine Interface (HMI)
- Sustainable Manufacturing
- Additive Manufacturing
- Product Research & Development (R&D)
specs: Developed at the Industrial Equipment Laboratory, Department of Mechanical Engineering, Vocational College, Universitas Gadjah Mada (UGM)
Converts discarded PET bottles into reusable 3D printing filament
Fully integrated bottle slicer, extruder, cooling, and automatic spooling system
PID-based extruder temperature control for stable processing
Multiple bottle slicing width options (6 mm, 8 mm, 10 mm, and 12 mm)
Automatic filament winding for neat and consistent spooling
Supports plastic waste recycling, circular economy, and SDGs initiatives
Designed as a research platform for sustainable manufacturing and additive manufacturing technologies
